page: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 128 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and single-core performance. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 21074.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 82984.7, placing it in the top tier of evaluated hardware. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block transfers reaching 197.26 GB/sec. The system also demonstrates high throughput in specialized tasks, processing 114.83 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 107.02 GB/sec in static web serving benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference processes prompts at 306.67 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

The c8i.16xlarge does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. Network connectivity is provisioned at a baseline speed of 30 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high compute density and memory speed relative to its resource profile, making it highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. Optimal workloads include software compilation, high-throughput web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, and parallel processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, achieving a Passmark CPU score of 82984.7 and a static web serving throughput of 107.02 GB/sec. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, making it highly efficient for vector and matrix computations. With a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-frequency compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
128 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated mathematical operations.
No local storage or dedicated GPU, relying on network-attached storage.
Best suited for software compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based inference.
